tweets.title: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully conducted a hot test of its semi-cryogenic engine power head, achieving 175 tonnes of thrust, around 88% of its target. The test, conducted at the ISRO Propulsion Complex, Mahendragiri, demonstrated stable performance and marks a key step toward a full 200-tonne thrust test. The new semi-cryogenic propulsion system, powered by liquid oxygen and kerosene, is being developed to replace the core stage of the LVM3 launch vehicle, significantly increasing payload capacity and improving launch efficiency.
tweets.content:
The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has successfully conducted a hot test of its semi-cryogenic engine power head, achieving 175 tonnes of thrust, around 88% of its target.

The test, conducted at the ISRO Propulsion Complex, Mahendragiri, demonstrated stable performance and marks a key step toward a full 200-tonne thrust test.

The new semi-cryogenic propulsion system, powered by liquid oxygen and kerosene, is being developed to replace the core stage of the LVM3 launch vehicle, significantly increasing payload capacity and improving launch efficiency.

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: 🚨 Three LeT Operatives Dead in Pakistan Amid Growing Mystery. A wave of unexplained deaths has struck Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) leadership in Pakistan, with three high-profile operatives dying within the span of just a few days. ♦️1, Ghazi Mumtaz: A veteran LeT trainer responsible for preparing countless recruits, Mumtaz died under mysterious circumstances in Upper Dir. ♦️2, Muhammad Khuzaimah Qasim: Killed in a hit-and-run case in Bahawalpur. Qasim was the brother of senior LeT leader Muhammad Yaqoob (Gen Sec of PMML - Mubarakpur); his funeral was presided over by Hafiz Abdul Mannan Makki, brother of LeT co-founder Abdul Rehman Makki. ♦️3, Khalid Bashir: A senior LeT operative reportedly found dead under suspicious conditions. Details surrounding his death remain under investigation. 📍The rapid succession of these deaths has left questions mounting within the organization’s ranks.
tweets.content:
🚨 Three LeT Operatives Dead in Pakistan Amid Growing Mystery.

A wave of unexplained deaths has struck Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT) leadership in Pakistan, with three high-profile operatives dying within the span of just a few days.

♦️1, Ghazi Mumtaz: A veteran LeT trainer responsible for preparing countless recruits, Mumtaz died under mysterious circumstances in Upper Dir.

♦️2, Muhammad Khuzaimah Qasim: Killed in a hit-and-run case in Bahawalpur. Qasim was the brother of senior LeT leader Muhammad Yaqoob (Gen Sec of PMML - Mubarakpur); his funeral was presided over by Hafiz Abdul Mannan Makki, brother of LeT co-founder Abdul Rehman Makki.

♦️3, Khalid Bashir: A senior LeT operative reportedly found dead under suspicious conditions. Details surrounding his death remain under investigation.

📍The rapid succession of these deaths has left questions mounting within the organization’s ranks.

tweets.title: BREAKING: Intelligence Alert Issued Over Possible Khalistani Terror Threat Intelligence agencies have issued a security alert for Delhi and Uttarakhand following inputs about a possible Khalistani terror threat. According to sources, temples, government offices, railway stations, and police installations have been identified as potential targets in a threat email. Security has been heightened across sensitive locations, while Delhi Police and intelligence agencies are investigating the email to verify its authenticity and trace its origin. The alert is precautionary, and investigations are ongoing.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Intelligence Alert Issued Over Possible Khalistani Terror Threat

Intelligence agencies have issued a security alert for Delhi and Uttarakhand following inputs about a possible Khalistani terror threat.

According to sources, temples, government offices, railway stations, and police installations have been identified as potential targets in a threat email.

Security has been heightened across sensitive locations, while Delhi Police and intelligence agencies are investigating the email to verify its authenticity and trace its origin. The alert is precautionary, and investigations are ongoing.

tweets.content:
🚨 Filter the Fiction

❌ Propaganda accounts are circulating false claims using an old video alleging that “a retired Indian Army officer Brigadier Surendra Kumar was caught in the UK in connection with a child sexual abuse case”.

✅ Fact: A review of the viral video and the previous article published by Desiblitz found no mention supporting this allegation. Additionally, no verified UK law enforcement statement, court record or credible media report has been found confirming that Brigadier Surendra Kumar was arrested, charged or convicted in such a case.

🎯 Disinformation Pattern of Pak based accounts like @ThokaReturns:
Unverified allegations → questionable sourcing → social media amplification → Disinformation spread.

⚠️ Verify before you amplify.

🔗 Cross-check claims with official police statements, court records and credible news organisations before sharing.

tweets.title: There are two types of airforces : Rich and Poor News : It’s official: F-35s are now being delivered without radars due to delays in the AN/APG-85 AESA radar program. It highlights two broad approaches to defence procurement. ✅ One approach is followed by countries that buy finished products. They expect complete, ready-to-fight systems because their military’s job is to fight wars, not participate in weapon development. ✅ The other approach is followed by countries that treat suppliers as long-term partners. They understand that complex defence programs will face engineering and supply-chain disruptions, especially during periods of heightened geopolitical tension. These airforces are “poor” and do not see importing an alternative at will is often an option, they work through the delays together rather than expecting perfection from day one.
tweets.content:
There are two types of airforces : Rich and Poor

News : It’s official: F-35s are now being delivered without radars due to delays in the AN/APG-85 AESA radar program.

It highlights two broad approaches to defence procurement.

✅ One approach is followed by countries that buy finished products. They expect complete, ready-to-fight systems because their military’s job is to fight wars, not participate in weapon development.

✅ The other approach is followed by countries that treat suppliers as long-term partners. They understand that complex defence programs will face engineering and supply-chain disruptions, especially during periods of heightened geopolitical tension.

These airforces are “poor” and do not see importing an alternative at will is often an option, they work through the delays together rather than expecting perfection from day one.

Kishtwar Incident: Army's Version of Events (Summary)

According to the Army's account, the incident began after Rashtriya Rifles (RR) civilian vehicles, engaged in official duties, inadvertently delayed the Deputy Commissioner's convoy by 10–20 minutes. Following the delay, the civil administration allegedly ordered the immediate seizure of the Army vehicles, which were taken to Atholi Police Station.

The Army claims the seizure was carried out without following the prescribed Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). In an effort to resolve the matter peacefully, the Commanding Officer reportedly sent a Company Commander and a Quick Reaction Team (QRT) to the police station to seek the release of the vehicles through dialogue.

The Army alleges that police personnel locked the station gates and declined repeated requests for discussion. It further claims that after attempts at communication failed, the gates were breached. During the confrontation, a police official allegedly cocked his weapon and threatened the Army personnel, prompting the QRT to intervene, disarm the individual, and secure the area. A physical altercation followed, involving police personnel, including the Station House Officer (SHO).

The Army maintains that the subsequent FIR filed against its personnel resulted from a breakdown in coordination between the civil administration and the Army. The matter remains under investigation, and the claims made by both sides have not been judicially established.

BREAKING: J&K Police Files FIR Against Rashtriya Rifles Personnel in Kishtwar Incident

Jammu & Kashmir Police have registered an FIR against the Commanding Officer of 17 Rashtriya Rifles, a Major, a Naib Subedar, and 30–40 Army personnel over an alleged incident at Atholi Police Station, Kishtwar, on June 24.

The FIR includes charges under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), including attempt to murder, and the Prevention of Damage to Public Property Act. Police allege Army personnel entered the police station, assaulted officers, and damaged government property.

The Indian Army has acknowledged the incident, said it is examining the matter through established institutional mechanisms, and stated it will fully cooperate with the investigation. The allegations are under investigation, and no judicial findings have been made.
